cases.label: Keyword Stacking
cases.query: Texas non-compete overbroad industry-wide restriction reformation employer breach

cases.label: Narrow Single Issue (Employer Breach)
cases.query: employer materially breached employment agreement by unilaterally reducing compensation then fired employee can non-compete still be enforced Texas

cases.label: Focused Legal Question
cases.query: enforceability of overbroad non-compete agreement that restricts work in entire industry rather than just competitors under Texas Business and Commerce Code Section 15.50

cases.label: Full Fact Pattern (Semantic/NLP)
cases.query: Texas employee signed 2-year non-compete covering 7 states with company. Employee was top salesman paid mostly commissions. Employer tried to force employee into salaried-only role at half pay. Employee refused, was fired for insubordination. Employee started new business in same industry but selling non-competitive product. Employer suing to enforce non-compete and seeking injunction plus damages. Non-compete defines competitors so broadly it covers entire industry not just actual competitors.

authorities.label: Descriptive Statute Query
authorities.query: Texas covenants not to compete act
